supersedes: an earlier draft of this file that claimed a 'list-slicing divergence' — that was a PRINT ARTIFACT (the fourth arm compares the band's RETURN value; a band ending in (print x) returns unit→0 while Go reports stdout). Corrected below with bare-return testing.
headline: fkwu DOES support float four-way. Careful four-way re-testing (bare-return verdicts) found TWO real divergences in this session's new bands, and corrected a false 'fkwu lacks float' claim.

float_supported_genuine_four_way.note: value also confirmed four-way via a bare-return value probe, not just the boolean

divergence_A_rust_multihead.observed: transformer-mh-band: go=1, rust=0, ts=1 — FAILS THREE-WAY. Value probe of tb-mh-block[0][0]*1e6: go=525305, ts=525305, rust=524669, fkwu=-5.
divergence_A_rust_multihead.diagnosis: Go and TS match numpy (the multi-head parity harness showed bin-go vs numpy = 1.9e-16), so Go/TS are correct and the RUST kernel computes the multi-head path ~6e-4 off. Single-head transformer-block is fully four-way (511), so the bug is specific to the multi-head split/concat path in the Rust kernel. fkwu additionally errors (-5).
divergence_A_rust_multihead.honest_admission: transformer-mh/decoder/generate were committed earlier THIS session tested via bin-go (Go only); they were never actually cross-kernel valid. The recipe is correct (Go/TS/numpy agree); the Rust kernel has the bug.
divergence_A_rust_multihead.status: hard gate — root-cause in the Rust kernel's handling of the multi-head path; not registered in the manifest until it crosses.

divergence_B_fkwu_only.diagnosis: Three-way solid; an isolated fourth-arm (fkwu) divergence. mel-frame (same trig family) crosses four-way, so it is not trig per se — to root-cause in the flattener/walker for these specific recipes.
divergence_B_fkwu_only.status: owned, tracked; three-way is the floor for these until fkwu crosses.

methodology_lessons: The fourth arm compares the band's RETURN value — bands must end in a bare verdict expression, never (print x).
Boolean verdicts can FALSE-PASS or be mis-read; verify with a bare-return VALUE probe, and always read go/rust/ts/fkwu separately, not just 'kernels agree'.
Validate every band three-way via validate.sh, never bin-go (Go) alone, before claiming it is proven.
